vix.note: CBOE VIX via FMP index-quote (free tier); auto-activates vix_spike_regime when px>=28 or day>=30

macro: Monday open setup: FOMC meeting June 16-17 approaches with no rate cuts priced in; market odds now favor a hike by year-end with the 10-year near 4.54%. The 10% blanket tariff terminates July 24, leaving markets to assess conversion to longer-term Section 301 tariffs. BTC rebounds +4.2% to $63,110 on Sunday while gold eases to $4,349; hyperscaler custom chip spend (~$725B capex across AWS, Alphabet, MSFT, Meta) keeps pressure on NVDA heading into Monday.
action: Hold positions into Monday's open; watch BTC at $64k resistance and monitor NVDA's reaction to hyperscaler chip competition news before adding exposure.

notes.NVDA: Hyperscaler risk: AWS, Alphabet, MSFT and Meta targeting ~$725B capex in 2026, scaling custom silicon to reduce NVDA dependence. Structural threat to NVDA data center revenue concentration heading into H2.
notes.MSFT: MSFT distancing from OpenAI seen as margin-positive; stock down 13% YTD but cloud AI build-out thesis intact. Ranked alongside AWS and Alphabet in best-buy cloud stock debate.

long.h: Hyperscalers race to build own AI chips; $725B capex puts NVDA customer concentration at risk
long.p: Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft and Meta scaling custom silicon to run more workloads internally. Combined ~$725B 2026 capex signals long-term risk to NVDA data center dominance.

long.h: Microsoft distancing from OpenAI is a positive for investors as MSFT stock down 13% in 2026
long.p: MSFT released its own reasoning AI models, reducing cost exposure to OpenAI. Analysts view independence as margin-positive; cloud AI thesis remains intact.

long.h: Which Big 3 cloud stock is the best buy? AI build-out benefits all three long-term
long.p: MSFT, AWS and Alphabet each offer distinct risk-reward in the AI infrastructure cycle. Cloud computing's structural role in AI workloads supports long-term growth across the sector.

long.h: USD falls 17% YTD; June 8 open faces dollar weakness and continued semi-sector pressure
long.p: Leveraged semiconductor ETFs face elevated volatility. Dollar weakness adds complexity to international earnings translation ahead of Monday open.

long.h: FOMC June 16-17 looms with zero cut odds; 10% blanket tariff cliff due July 24
long.p: Fed target range at 3.50-3.75%; markets now price possible 2026 hike. Tariff expiry July 24 raises risk of conversion to Section 301 tariffs, sustaining inflation pressure into H2.

short.h: Bitcoin rebounds to $63,110 on June 7, up 4.2% from prior close of $60,562
short.p: BTC recovered on Sunday after a rough week. Near-term resistance at $64k-$65k where short positions are clustered; sustained break above would shift near-term bias positive.

short.h: Traders on Kalshi see 80% chance BTC falls below $60k; new 2026 lows forecast
short.p: CNBC reports prediction market odds heavily bearish. May spot ETF outflows of $2.30B were the largest monthly bleed since November 2025, removing key buying pressure.

short.h: May 2026 BTC ETF net outflows hit $2.30B, worst monthly bleed since Nov 2025
short.p: Institutional BTC demand cooling as ETF flows turn sharply negative. Sustained outflows raise tail risk if $60k support is lost in June.

short.h: Standard Chartered holds $100k BTC year-end target; Bernstein forecasts $150k
short.p: Bull case rests on institutional re-accumulation and potential Fed pivot. June median BTC return is +2.58% historically, but macro headwinds are unusually strong this year.

short.h: BTC fell below $72k in early June on $2.97B ETF outflows; recovering but range-bound
short.p: Bitcoin has spent mid-2026 in a wide range below post-halving highs. Recovery from $59k lows is constructive but ETF flow data suggests caution on adding long exposure.
